o-pesto-ever.name: O Pesto Ever
instructions: Pulse pine nuts, almonds, and garlic in a food processor for about 20 seconds. Add basil, 1 cup olive oil, salt, and pepper. Blend for about 1 minute. Add additional olive oil until desired consistency is reached.

ingredients.item: 1 cda of stevia or sweetener of your choice

instructions: En un recipiente agrega la harina, avena, stevia, polvo para hornear, canela y sal. Mezcla bien.
En otro recipiente agrega el huevo, la mantequilla derretida y la esencia de vainilla y mezcla.
It incorporates the dry ingredients into the fluids little by little and mixes with a tablespoon to gain a mass.
Una vez este lista la masa, agrega las nueces picadas y amasa nuevamente hasta integrar muy bien el fruto seco.
Take small portions and shape balls, place them on a baking plate with oven paper and leave space between them. Press with your hand to put up with a uniform light to form a disc.

Hornea at 182 been 3 been for 10 to 15 minutes. When they are ready they will be golden by the bottom. Remove them and let them cool before withdrawing them from the plate to prevent them from breaking.

off-the-wagon.name: Off The Wagon

ingredients.prep: cut into wedges, plus 4 wedges for garnish

instructions: Prepare grill for medium-high heat. Grill plums until caramelized and lightly charred, about 1 minute per side; let cool.

Muddle 2 plums in a large pitcher until coarsely mashed. Add bourbon, vermouth, lemon juice, honey, and bitters and fill pitcher with ice. Stir until outside of pitcher is frosty, about 1 minute. Strain into 4 Old Fashioned glasses filled with ice; discard solids. Garnish each with a plum wedge.

old-bay-potatoes.name: Old Bay Potatoes
instructions: Fill a large pot halfway with water and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to medium-low and add garlic, seafood seasoning, and thyme. Let cook for 2 minutes; add potatoes. Drain some of the water mixture so that there is a thin layer just hovering over potatoes.
Let cook over low to medium heat until tender, about 10 minutes. Drain and serve.

old-fashioned-apple-dumplings.name: Old-fashioned Apple Dumplings
instructions: Roll pastry into a 24x16-inch rectangle on a lightly floured surface. Cut into six 8-inch squares. Place an apple on each pastry square with the cored opening facing upward.
Cut butter into 8 pieces; place 1 piece in the opening of each apple; reserve the remaining butter for sauce.
Divide brown sugar between apples, poking some inside each cored opening and the rest around the base of each apple. Sprinkle cinnamon and nutmeg over apples.

Using slightly wet fingertips, bring one corner of the pastry square up to the top of the apple, then bring the opposite corner to the top and press together. Bring up the two remaining corners, and seal. Slightly pinch the dough at the sides to completely seal in the apple. Repeat with the remaining apples and place in the prepared baking dish.

To make the sauce: Combine water, white sugar, vanilla extract, and reserved butter in a large saucepan. Place over medium heat, and bring to a boil. Reduce the heat to low and simmer until sugar is dissolved, about 5 minutes.
Carefully pour the sauce over dumplings.
Bake in the preheated oven until golden brown and crisp on top, about 50 to 60 minutes.
Place each apple dumpling in a dessert bowl, and spoon some of the sauce over the top.

olive-oil-galette-with-spicy-greens.name: Olive Oil Galette With Spicy Greens

ingredients.prep: white and pale green parts, halved lengthwise, sliced crosswise on a diagonal 1–1 0.5”-thick, or 4 scallions, coarsely chopped
ribs and stems removed, leaves torn into bite-size pieces

ingredients.item: Full-fat greek yogurt (optional) and coarsely chopped tender herbs (such as dill

instructions: Whisk flour, sugar, and salt in a large bowl to combine. Whisk together yogurt and oil in a small bowl (it won’t be perfectly smooth). Make a well in center of dry ingredients and scrape in yogurt mixture. Using a fork, gradually incorporate dry ingredients into yogurt mixture, then use your hands to work together until a shaggy dough forms (you can also turn out onto a surface if that’s easier). If the dough doesn’t come together in 1 piece, knead in 1 Tbsp. ice water. Shape dough into a 1”-thick disk. Wrap in plastic and chill at least 2 hours and up to 12 hours.

Heat oil in a large skillet over medium. Add leek and cook, stirring, until softened and wilted (don’t let it take on any color), 6–8 minutes. Add garlic and red pepper flakes and cook, stirring, just until garlic begins to soften, about 1 minute. Add Swiss chard by the handful, letting each addition wilt slightly before adding more; cook, stirring often, just until chard is tender, about 4 minutes. Remove from heat; season with salt and pepper. Transfer Swiss chard mixture to a large bowl and let cool 20 minutes. Mix Parmesan into filling.

Preheat oven to 400°. Unwrap dough and roll out on a lightly floured sheet of parchment paper to a 12” round about ⅛” thick. Slide dough and parchment onto a baking sheet. Scatter filling over dough, leaving a 1½” border. Bring edges of dough up and over filling, overlapping as needed; brush dough with egg. Chill in freezer 10 minutes.
Bake galette, rotating front to back halfway through, until crust is golden brown and cooked through, 40–50 minutes. Let cool slightly on baking sheet.
To serve, dollop filling with yogurt if desired and top with herbs and lemon zest.

one-pot-chicken-and-vegetable-roast.name: One-pot Chicken And Vegetable Roast
instructions: Preheat the oven to 204°C (200 degrees C).
Combine butternut squash, potatoes, carrot, 1/4 cup olive oil, lemon pepper seasoning, and 1/2 teaspoon salt in a bowl; toss to combine. Transfer vegetables to a baking sheet.
Toss chicken breasts in the same bowl with 1 tablespoon olive oil, remaining salt, and Caribbean seasoning until coated. Add to baking sheet.

Bake in the preheated oven until chicken is no longer pink in the center, juices run clear, and vegetables are tender, about 25 minutes. An instant-read thermometer inserted into the center should read at least 74°C (74 degrees C).

orange-slushie-for-a-fresh-and-healthy-d.name: Orange Slushie For A Fresh And Healthy Drink
orange-slushie-for-a-fresh-and-healthy-d.original_name: Granizado de naranja casero para una bebida fresca y saludable
instructions: Exprime el jugo de las naranjas y reserva.
In a pot, put water and sugar and heat up at medium heat till the sugar dissolve. Then let the mixture cool.
Add the orange juice to the water and sugar mixture and remove well. Fold the mixture into an ice plate and frost for at least 4 hours.
Once the mixture is frozen, remove the plate from the freezer and put the ice cubes into a blender. Add the chopped and mix to get a granulated consistency.
Sirve el granizado de naranja en vasos y disfruta.

orange-ginger-pickled-baby-carrots.name: Orange-ginger Pickled Baby Carrots

ingredients.prep: peeled and trimmed, leaving 1/2 inch of stems intact; or 2 lb medium carrots

instructions: Blanch carrots in a nonreactive 4- to 5-qt heavy pot of salted boiling water, stirring occasionally, 1 minute. Transfer carrots to an ice bath to stop cooking, then drain in a colander and transfer to a heatproof bowl

Bring vinegar, orange zest and juice, chiles, ginger, sugar, and 1 1/2 tsp salt to a boil in same pot, stirring until sugar has dissolved. Pour over carrots and cool, uncovered, stirring occasionally, about 1 hour.
Chill carrots in pickling liquid in an airtight container, shaking container occasionally, at least 1 day.
